# velia_ticket (Resource)

Opens a support ticket with Velia. Tickets cannot be deleted via the API; destroying this resource only removes it from Terraform state.

All input fields (`topic`, `subject`, `message`, `servers`) force replacement when changed, since tickets are immutable after creation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "velia_ticket" "example" {
  topic   = "velianet-support"
  subject = "Network connectivity issue on server 12345"
  message = "Since 10:00 UTC we are experiencing packet loss on eth0."
  servers = [12345]
}
```

## Import

Import using the ticket ID:

```shell
terraform import velia_ticket.example 315024
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `message` (String) Full description of the issue.
- `subject` (String) Brief summary of the issue (max 200 characters).
- `topic` (String) Topic queue: velianet-billing, velianet-sales, velianet-reinstall, velianet-setup, or velianet-support.

### Optional

- `servers` (List of Number) Server IDs to associate with the ticket.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Ticket ID.
- `queue` (String) Queue the ticket was routed to.
- `status` (String) Current ticket status.
